strengths: Achieved excellent completion time of 22 seconds with zero penalties
Demonstrated smooth bilateral coordination throughout all transfer phases
Maintained consistent instrument control during complex mid-air exchanges
Successfully completed all outbound and return transfers without object drops
weaknesses: Occasional hesitation during instrument repositioning between transfers
Minor inefficiencies in grasp optimization during rapid succession transfers
Could refine economy of motion during transition between transfer directions

drills.name: Economy-of-motion ladder
drills.description: Perform complete peg transfer sequences in slow motion, focusing on minimal instrument repositioning and optimal grasp angles throughout.
drills.targets_weakness: Minor inefficiencies in grasp optimization during rapid succession transfers.

drills.name: Continuous flow rehearsal
drills.description: Practice seamless transitions between outbound and return transfer phases without pausing, emphasizing fluid instrument movement.
drills.targets_weakness: Occasional hesitation during instrument repositioning between transfers.

progression_note: At the advanced level, focus on achieving sub-20 second times while maintaining this excellent penalty-free performance. Your current technique shows mastery of the fundamental skills - now optimize for maximum efficiency and benchmark pursuit.
